Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induced at 0.2 mM in adventitious roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate associated with triterpene saponin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. One of a few B. chinense UGT candidates proposed for saikosaponin biosynthesis. 2. Root-enriched and MeJA-inducible. 3. Catalytic activity not functionally verified in this review.

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induced at 0.2 mM in adventitious roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate associated with triterpene saponin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate saikosaponin-related UGT in B. chinense. 2. Root-biased and MeJA-responsive. 3. Not functionally characterized biochemically.

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induced at 0.2 mM in adventitious roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate associated with triterpene saponin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate UGT proposed for saikosaponin biosynthesis. 2. Root-enriched and MeJA-inducible. 3. Lacks biochemical validation in the review.

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induced at 0.1 mM in hairy roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate associated with triterpene saponin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeJA-inducible C. asiatica UGT candidate. 2. Proposed to participate in triterpene saponin metabolism. 3. No direct enzymatic function reported in the review.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots followed by leaves and flower
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Early MeJA responsive; 0.05 mM in adventitious roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate linked to ginsenoside biosynthesis induction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeJA-responsive PgUGT candidate in Group E. 2. Tissue expression enriched in roots. 3. Biochemical activity toward ginsenosides not established in review.

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Responds early within first 6 h of MeJA treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate linked to MeJA-induced ginsenoside biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Early MeJA-responsive Panax UGT candidate. 2. Group E behavior resembles ginsenoside-related UGT71 candidates. 3. Catalytic function not biochemically resolved.
1. Group A candidate induced by MeJA. 2. Leaf-expressed in P. ginseng. 3. No direct catalytic evidence reported.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots followed by leaves and flower
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Panax candidate UGT induced by MeJA. 2. Tissue-biased toward roots. 3. Catalytic function not directly shown.
1. Flower-bud expressed Group A candidate. 2. MeJA inducible. 3. No biochemical activity reported.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots followed by leaves and flower
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tissue-expressed Panax UGT candidate. 2. Included among putative triterpene-related UGTs. 3. Function remains unvalidated.
1. Putative Group E Panax UGT. 2. Leaf-expressed. 3. No catalytic characterization reported.
1. Group L candidate in Panax. 2. MeJA inducible and leaf-expressed. 3. Catalytic role unresolved.
1. Leaf-expressed Panax UGT candidate. 2. Group A candidate implicated by expression patterns. 3. Not biochemically validated.
1. Putative Group O candidate UGT in Panax. 2. Leaf expressed. 3. Function not resolved.

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA 0.1 mM in hairy roots; 7 days
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay in hairy roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Unidentified UGT activity induced by MeJA in hairy roots. 2. Catalyzes conversion of Rh2 to Rg3. 3. Supports pathway-level induction of PPD-type ginsenosides.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity reported without specific gene identity.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D, PSPG motif-containing GT-B fold enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanolic acid; Hederagenin; Lupane sapogenin; Betulinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 β-D-glucosylation of triterpene sapogenins including oleanolic acid and hederagenin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense including insecticidal and antifungal activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functionally characterized as a Group D UGT from Barbarea vulgaris. 2. Catalyzes conserved C-3 glucosylation on multiple triterpene sapogenins. 3. Contributes to diversification of feeding-deterrent triterpenoid saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of C-3 glucosylated triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes this as a known triterpene-related UGT with conserved regioselectivity across substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway engineering of C-3 glucosylated defense-related saponins and regioselective biocatalysis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanolic acid; Hederagenin; Lupane sapogenin; Betulinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 β-D-glucosylation of multiple triterpene sapogenins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense including insecticidal and antifungal activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Group D UGT with broad substrate range. 2. Specifically glycosylates C-3 position of oleanolic acid/hederagenin and related sapogenins. 3. Supports conserved regioselectivity in B. vulgaris saponin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-3 glucosylated triterpene saponin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for engineering triterpene glycodiversification

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-28 ester-forming glycosylation of oleanane/lupane sapogenins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanolic acid; Hederagenin; Lupane sapogenin; Betulinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-28 β-D-glucosylation forming ester linkage on triterpene sapogenins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Group D UGT catalyzing C-28 glucosylation. 2. Acts on oleanolic acid, hederagenin, and related sapogenins. 3. Demonstrates conserved positional specificity distinct from C-3 glucosylating paralogs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-28 glucosylated triterpenoid saponin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ester-linked C-28 saponins

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-28 ester-forming glycosylation of oleanane/lupane sapogenins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanolic acid; Hederagenin; Lupane sapogenin; Betulinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-28 β-D-glucosylation on triterpene sapogenins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Group D UGT for C-28 glucosylation. 2. Shares substrates with BvUGT73C12 but preserves C-28 regioselectivity. 3. Expands glucosylated saponin diversity in B. vulgaris.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-28 glucosylated triterpenoid saponin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro enzymatic assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Asiatic acid glucoside; Madecassic acid glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Attaches glucose to the C-28 carboxyl moiety of asiatic acid and madecassic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues, highest in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA responsive in hairy roots (reviewed for related C. asiatica UGTs)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functionally characterized as a C-28 glucosyltransferase in C. asiatica. 2. Uses asiatic acid and madecassic acid as substrates. 3. Contributes to centelloside-type saponin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for metabolic engineering of medicinal centellosides

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-28 β-D-glucosylation of asiatic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate/characterized Group D UGT responsive to MeJA. 2. Glycosylates asiatic acid at C-28 to produce a monoglucoside. 3. Likely contributes to asiaticoside pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis plus enzymatic assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glucose to the arabinose residue of soyasapogenol A-derived substrate at C-22
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Soybean Group D UGT with C-22 β-D-glucosylation activity. 2. Converts saponin A0-αg to soyasaponin Ab. 3. Together with UGT73F4 controls terminal sugar diversity at C-22.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene mapping/functional characterization and in vitro assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds xylose to the arabinose residue of soyasapogenol A-derived substrate at C-22
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Soybean Group D UGT with C-22 β-D-xylosylation activity. 2. Converts saponin A0-αg to soyasaponin Aa. 3. Contributes to terminal sugar diversity of group A saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization and in vitro assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, Group A-related in review text though named UGT73P2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds galactose as the second sugar to soyasapogenol B 3-O-glucuronide at C-3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Catalyzes second sugar addition in soybean soyasaponin pathway. 2. Converts soyasapogenol B 3-O-glucuronide to soyasaponin III. 3. Exemplifies sequential oligosaccharide assembly by plant triterpene UGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization and in vitro assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT91 family, Group A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds rhamnose as the third sugar to soyasaponin III at C-3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Catalyzes successive sugar addition rather than first sugar attachment. 2. Converts soyasaponin III to soyasaponin II via C-3 rhamnosylation. 3. Conserved with other Group A enzymes performing later sugar additions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization and in vitro assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, Group A
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Ginsenoside CK; Ginsenoside F2; Ginsenoside Rd; Ginsenoside F1
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Gynenoside LXXV; Gynenoside XVII; Ginsenoside Rb1; Notoginsenoside U
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the C-20 position of PPD- or PPT-type ginsenosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Conserved C-20 regioselective glucosyltransferase from Gynostemma. 2. Accepts multiple PPD/PPT-type ginsenoside substrates. 3. Produces diversified downstream gypenoside products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Patent-described functional characterization and enzymatic assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT71 family, Group E, GT-B fold with PSPG motif
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis; also flavonoid glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the C-3 hydroxyl of medicagenic acid; also shows flavonoid activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multifunctional UGT acting on both triterpenoids and flavonoids in vitro. 2. Functions in elicited cultures correlate with triterpenoid rather than flavonoid glycoside accumulation. 3. Crystal structure defined catalytic residues and substrate-binding features relevant to UGT engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes medicagenic acid glucoside formation and triterpenoid saponin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression, in vitro assay, elicited cell culture analysis, crystal structure, docking
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad in vitro substrate range may not reflect in vivo role due to substrate availability.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important scaffold for enzyme engineering of UGT regioselectivity and sugar specificity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes β-D-glucosylation of multiple sapogenins at several positions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functionally characterized Group D UGT with substrate promiscuity. 2. Acts on hederagenin and soyasapogenols B/E. 3. Contributes to glycosylation diversity in M. truncatula saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates hederagenin at the C-28 position
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Microsomal Group D UGT active on multiple sapogenins. 2. Known to glycosylate hederagenin at C-28. 3. Predicted to relate to soybean UGT73F2/F4 substrate branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microsomal enzyme characterization and in vitro assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT74 family, Group L
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Esterifies a hexose with the C-28 carboxyl group of hederagenin/α-hederin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induced in seedlings at 0.1 mM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Group L UGT catalyzing C-28 glucosylation. 2. Expressed broadly with peak in leaves and capsules. 3. Supports biosynthesis of mono- and bidesmosidic saponins in N. sativa.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-28 hexosylated triterpene saponin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression in yeast and enzymatic assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT85 family, Group G
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sugar to hydroxyl group of C-3 in hederagenin; table also notes C3 or C24 glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in capsules, flowers, leaves; no expression in root
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induced in seedlings at 0.1 mM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Group G UGT characterized by recombinant expression in yeast. 2. Responsible for glucosylation of hederagenin. 3. MeJA inducible and tissue-specific in N. sativa.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant yeast expression and enzymatic assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT74 family, Group L
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 β-D-glucosylation of protopanaxadiol and compound K
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ginsenosides Rh2/F2 and downstream PPD ginsenosides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA responsive; reviewed among root/leaf-expressed PgUGTs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA 0.2 mM induces expression with upstream terpenoid genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Catalyzes first characterized C-3 glycosylation step from PPD/CK to Rh2/F2. 2. Works with PgUGT94Q2 in sequential formation of Rg3/Rd. 3. MeJA-inducible and implicated in PPD-type ginsenoside enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Rh2 and F2 biosynthesis and channels flux to PPD-type ginsenosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical assay, expression analysis under MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering medicinal PPD-type ginsenoside production

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT94 family, Group A
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PPD-type ginsenoside C-3 second sugar addition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to C-3 of Rh2 and F2 to produce Rg3 and Rd
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA 0.2 mM; implicated in ~100-fold Rg3 increase in adventitious roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Catalyzes second glucose addition at C-3 in PPD-type ginsenoside biosynthesis. 2. Converts Rh2/F2 to Rg3/Rd. 3. MeJA-inducible and associated with stimulated Rg3 accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical assay and MeJA expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for Rg3/Rd enrichment in ginseng engineering

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT71 family, Group E
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dammarendiol-II; Protopanaxadiol; Protopanaxatriol; Ginsenoside Rh2; Ginsenoside Rd
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 20S-O-β-(D-glucosyl)-dammarendiol II; Ginsenoside CK; Ginsenoside F1; Ginsenoside F2; Ginsenoside Rd
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates the C-20 position of dammarane-type aglycones and ginsenosides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA responsive in roots and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Relaxed-substrate Group E glucosyltransferase acting on both PPD- and PPT-type substrates. 2. Catalyzes C-20 glucosylation of dammarenediol, PPD, PPT and ginsenoside intermediates. 3. Important in MeJA-responsive ginsenoside pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes multiple C-20 glucosylated ginsenoside products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical assay and MeJA-responsive expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable broad-specificity enzyme for ginsenoside pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, Group E
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-6 β-D-glucosylation of protopanaxatriol to form Rh1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Group E UGT catalyzing PPT C-6 glucosylation. 2. Produces ginsenoside Rh1 from protopanaxatriol. 3. Defines a distinct PPT-branch decorating step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, Group E
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-20 and C-6 β-D-glucosylation of PPT-type substrates, enabling sequential formation of F1 and Rg1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dual-function Group E UGT acting at both C-6 and C-20. 2. Produces F1 and subsequently Rg1. 3. Demonstrates multifunctional glycosylation in PPT branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT94 family, Group A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 glucosylation of Rh2 and F2 to produce Rg3 and Rd
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues, highest in flowers (for related contigs)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RNAi reduced total ginsenosides; MeJA-related expression similarity with DS in P. quinquefolius contigs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functional ortholog of PgUGT94Q2 in American ginseng. 2. RNAi reduces Rd, PPD-type and total ginsenosides. 3. Catalytic activity conserved before Panax species radiation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Rg3/Rd and total PPD-type ginsenoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference and enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for enhancing PPD-type ginsenosides in Panax breeding/engineering

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT74 family, Group L
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 β-D-glucosylation of protopanaxadiol to form Rh2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functional ortholog of PgUGT74AE2 in P. quinquefolius. 2. Catalyzes C-3 glucosylation of protopanaxadiol. 3. Shows evolutionarily conserved activity within Panax.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization and enzymatic assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT74 family, Group L
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates the C-3 hydroxyl group of mogrol to form mogroside IE
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweetness determinant and medicinal cucurbitane saponin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq-guided candidate confirmed biochemically. 2. Heterologously expressed His-tag enzyme specifically glycosylates mogrol at C-3. 3. Important for mogroside biosynthesis during fruit ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/differential expression, heterologous expression in E. coli, enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for sweetness-related mogroside engineering in monk fruit

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT family, Group G per table
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 and C-24 glucosylation steps in mogroside pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as mogroside-pathway UGT from S. grosvenorii. 2. Functions at both C-3 and C-24 positions. 3. Contributes to sequential mogroside glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization summarized in review

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT94 family, Group A
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Mogroside IIE; Mogroside IIIx; Mogroside IV-A or Siamenoside
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Mogroside IIIx; Mogroside IV-A or Siamenoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes additional glucose transfer steps on mogroside intermediates at C-3 and C-24
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functions in later sequential glycosylation of mogrosides. 2. Accepts already glycosylated mogroside intermediates. 3. Supports sweetness-associated mogroside diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes more highly glycosylated mogroside products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization summarized in review

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT74 family, Group L
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosyl ester formation at C-28 of gypsogenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Full-length cDNA isolated from developing seed ESTs. 2. Heterologously expressed enzyme glycosylates gypsogenic acid at C-28. 3. Downstream pathway shows organ-specific regulation distinct from upstream β-AS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-28 glycosylated oleanane saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EST isolation, heterologous expression, enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ester-linked Quillaja-like saponins

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of ginsenoside Rb1 from Rd by glycosylation at C-20
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity increases after MeJA treatment in P. ginseng hairy roots per review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Catalyzes C-20 glycosylation converting Rd to Rb1. 2. Represents a later step in Panax ginsenoside diversification. 3. Activity is discussed in relation to MeJA-induced pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT73 family, Group D
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Pentacyclic triterpenoids with C-30/C-29 carboxyl group
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers multiple hexose or pentose donors to the C-30/C-29 carboxyl group of pentacyclic triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. First recognized oleanane C-30 ester-forming glycosyltransferase. 2. Has strict acceptor specificity but broad sugar-donor promiscuity. 3. Offers a new biocatalytic route for glycosyl ester saponin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glycosyl ester formation on pentacyclic triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as the first reported example of triterpene ester-forming UGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value biocatalyst for synthetic production of novel glycosyl ester saponins
